untranslatable_reason: Signal requires identification of 'previously identified support/resistance levels' and tracking of 'successive test counts' (2-3 times). These require dynamic level detection and multi-bar memory logic not available in single-bar indicator set. Pivot points (s1/r1) used as proxy, but true implementation requires custom level-tracking state machine and test-count accumulation across multiple bars.
translation_notes: Attempted approximation using pivot point levels (s1/r1) as support/resistance proxies. Volume comparison (volume < prev_volume) represents 'weaker momentum on approach.' However, the core signal logic—detecting weakening of levels across multiple successive tests—cannot be fully expressed without custom state tracking. A backtest engine would need: (1) dynamic S/R level detection, (2) test counter per level, (3) strength degradation metric across tests. Recommend implementing as custom indicator or state-machine rule in backtest harness.
